evidence: VisualizationHandlers.handleVisualRadial (lines 45, 54): logger.debug("Radial: body = {}", body) dumps the full raw request body, followed immediately by logger.debug("Radial: center = {}", center) for each parsed value — diagnostic trace pair left from development.

CollocateProfileComparator: six debug-level statements inside a single method, including logger.debug("Nouns: {}", seedNouns) dumping the full input collection, per-item loop traces logger.debug("  {} (in {}/{} nouns, avg={})", p.adjective(), ...) and logger.debug("  {} -> {} ({})", ...) — constitutes noisy full-object-dump tracing across a core algorithmic method.

GrammarConfigLoader private helpers carry single-line Javadocs that restate the method name: '/** Extracts and validates the 'version' field from the root JSON object. */' on parseAndValidateVersion; '/** Rejects deprecated top-level keys that must not appear in the config. */' on validateNoLegacyKeys — both add zero information beyond the method signature.

RelationPatternBuilder.buildFullPattern: Objects.requireNonNull(config.pattern(), ...) is applied to a field declared @NonNull on the RelationConfig record — null guard on a typed non-null accessor is mildly defensive.

SemanticFieldExplorer contains a long class-level Javadoc with a worked example (Exploring from 'house'), numbered algorithm steps, multiple <h2> sections — this is appropriate for a central algorithmic facade. The Javadoc on the package-private testing-seam overload of exploreByPattern is also purposeful, explaining the design contract for tests. These are NOT issues.

strengths: Error handling is centralised in HttpApiUtils.wrapWithErrorHandling — no boilerplate try-catch blocks copied verbatim across handlers.
Domain terminology is used consistently throughout (lemma, deprel, collocate, logDice, xpos, BCQL) — no generic handleData/processItem placeholders.

Non-trivial design decisions carry purposeful explanatory comments: the @Nullable vs Optional choice in RelationConfig.deriveDeprel, the two-tier exception contract in createDefaultEnglish, and the testing-seam rationale on the package-private exploreByPattern overload all explain WHY, not just WHAT.

handleSurfaceRelations and handleDepRelations, while thin, are legitimate routing entry points that pin a public verb to a specific RelationType constant rather than leaking enum knowledge into callers.
SemanticFieldExplorer's algorithmic debug logs (entry parameters, fallback notice, completion summary) are informational progress markers, not blind entry/exit traces.
ai_generated_debt.issue_character: Issues are localised to diagnostic tracing left in from development: VisualizationHandlers dumps the full request body at debug level, and CollocateProfileComparator has six debug-trace statements including per-item loop dumps; a secondary minor pattern of restating single-line Javadocs appears on private helper methods in GrammarConfigLoader.
ai_generated_debt.score_rationale: The codebase sits in the solid-but-uneven range (80 anchor) pulled down by two distinct debug-trace clusters that exhibit the nosy-logging hallmark: full object dumps at debug level in a handler and per-item loop traces in a core algorithmic class. The restating private-method comments and one unnecessary null check are smaller symptoms of the same pattern. However, there is no systemic boilerplate, no generic naming, no redundant wrapper functions, and the more architecturally significant comments are genuinely purposeful — so the debt is real but localized, warranting a score around 73 rather than the 60 anchor.

issues.summary: VisualizationHandlers logs full request body and every parsed intermediate value at debug level
evidence: Line 45: logger.debug("Radial: body = {}", body) — dumps entire raw request JSON including all items array
Line 54: logger.debug("Radial: center = {}", center) — logs a single parsed scalar immediately after parsing it, providing no operational value beyond the body dump already emitted
issues.suggestion: Remove both debug statements; if request tracing is needed, keep only a single structured log with relevant identifiers (e.g., center value and item count), not raw body content.

issues.summary: CollocateProfileComparator emits six debug-level traces including per-item loop dumps across a single method
evidence: Line 66: logger.debug("Nouns: {}", seedNouns) dumps full input collection at method entry
Lines 163-175: two debug statements inside result-iteration loops, logging every adjective entry with formatted scores — O(n) log lines at debug level
issues.suggestion: Replace with at most two summary-level debug statements (entry with seed count, exit with result count). Remove per-item loop traces entirely; they produce unbounded output proportional to corpus size.

issues.summary: Two private helper methods in GrammarConfigLoader carry single-line Javadocs that restate the method name
evidence: '/** Extracts and validates the 'version' field from the root JSON object. */' on parseAndValidateVersion — duplicates information already in the method name
'/** Rejects deprecated top-level keys that must not appear in the config. */' on validateNoLegacyKeys — likewise
issues.suggestion: Remove both one-liner Javadocs; the method names are self-describing. Retain comments only if there is non-obvious intent to explain (e.g., why the legacy key is rejected rather than silently ignored).

root_causes: Debug diagnostic traces added during initial development were not pruned before the code stabilised — common in LLM-assisted drafts where verbose logging is generated as scaffolding.
Javadoc was generated on every private method regardless of whether it adds insight, a hallmark of completionist documentation style from code generation tools.
